what_it_does: A self-contained acceptance test for a code-symbol reverse indexer (symbols.py). It uses inline source fixtures and deterministic seam functions to verify that many code-reference forms resolve to symbols, that multiple owners pointing at the same symbol collapse into one reverse-index entry, that absent symbols and missing files surface as drift, and that optional semantic nearest-neighbor edges attach correctly and degrade gracefully when the embedder is unavailable.
observations: Inline fixture arrays PY, TSX, and BRIDGE simulate repo files without disk or network access.
fake_resolver canonicalizes bare and qualified paths (e.g., suite.py and runtime/suite.py) to the same repo file.
Tests path:symbol, path:line, file-only, Suite.verb, /api route, glob, directory, and compound reference forms.
Absent symbols and missing code files produce resolves:false as drift signals; non-code or unresolvable non-code refs return None.
symbol_id is stable and keyed off the resolved file stem plus symbol.
build_index merges multiple owners referencing the same canonical symbol into one entry with a referenced_by list.
shared_symbols filters entries referenced by two or more owners.
Reuses refcheck.collect_refs and expects a malformed registry to raise ValueError.
X11 semantic-edge tests use a concept-word fake_embed to produce deterministic, hermetic vectors.
attach_semantic_edges adds semantically_nearest[] entries pointing to other code:// ids with cosine-like scores.
The embedder-down path emits a warning, omits semantically_nearest entirely, and leaves the structural index intact.
build_registry produces a document with _what, symbols, and summary, and summary.semantic_edges reflects whether semantic edges were attached.
standouts: The deterministic fake-embedder acceptance test for semantic nearest-neighbor ordering without network access.
Reverse-index unification of bare vs qualified paths and compound references onto one canonical symbol.
Explicit graceful-degradation test for embedder failure.
conventions: Top docstring states purpose, run command, and key invariants.
Uses sys.path.insert to import sibling modules symbols and refcheck.
Fixture line numbers are 1-based and annotated with expected symbols.
Hermetic: no disk access, no external network, no heavy mocking beyond seam functions.
Assert messages include the actual value for loud failures.
Kind values are lower-case strings: def, class, const, route, file-only.
concerns: The file imports symbols.py and refcheck.py, which are not shown here, so it cannot run standalone without those siblings.
Several assertions depend on an API surface and exact return shapes (e.g., symbols._validate, symbols.attach_semantic_edges, symbols.representative_text) not defined in this file.
The fake_embed concept-word scheme is coarse and may not capture real embedding geometry.
The test assumes a specific symbol_id scheme and document schema without defining them locally.
notes: A hermetic acceptance test that pins the contract of a code-symbol reverse indexer, covering resolution forms, owner collapse, drift signals, and optional semantic nearest-neighbor edges with graceful embedder-down degradation.
questions: What exact schema does symbols.build_registry return and what does symbols._validate enforce?
How does symbols.py parse and split compound references such as 'scheduler.py:run / runtime/suite.py:run'?
What vector dimension and similarity threshold does the live BGE-M3 embedder path use?
Does the real resolver canonicalize bare vs qualified paths the same way fake_resolver does?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to test symbols.py's reverse index and semantic-edge behavior; the file does exactly that with inline fixtures and deterministic seams, so it matches.
apparent_themes: Graceful degradation of optional semantic features
intention_signals: It tries to lock down the contract of a universal code-coordinate reverse index so that many downstream owners can be collapsed onto one symbol, while leaving room for optional semantic similarity edges that survive embedder outages.
novelty: The combination of a reverse-symbol index test with a deterministic, concept-word fake embedder to prove semantic ordering without network access is unusual.
contribution: A concrete, runnable acceptance suite that documents expected behavior of code-symbol indexing, reverse-owner merging, drift classification, and semantic-edge attachment, including failure-mode behavior.
summary_for_embedding: Self-contained acceptance test for a code-symbol reverse indexer using inline source fixtures, a fake resolver, and a deterministic fake embedder to verify reference resolution, owner collapse, drift signals, and optional semantic nearest-neighbor edges.
intention_for_embedding: To pin the behavior of the symbols.py reverse-index module and its optional semantic-edge feature with loud, hermetic, no-network tests.

symbols.does: Maps requested paths to inline fixture source lines and canonical repo-relative paths.
symbols.description: Stand-in resolver used by tests to avoid disk access. It unifies bare and qualified paths such as suite.py and runtime/suite.py to the same repo file, returning a tuple of (repo-relative-path, lines).

symbols.does: Executes the full acceptance test suite for the symbols indexer.
symbols.description: Top-level test runner that asserts behavior of index_ref, symbol_id, build_index reverse merging, shared_symbols, malformed-registry handling, build_registry document shape, and X11 semantic nearest-neighbor edges including embedder-up and embedder-down degradation paths.

symbols.does: Deterministically embeds text by concept-word presence.
symbols.description: Hermetic stand-in for a BGE-M3 embedder; returns a vector with one axis per concept word from a fixed vocabulary, producing near-parallel vectors for conceptually similar texts and near-orthogonal vectors for unrelated texts.

symbols.does: Custom exception used to simulate an unreachable embedder.
symbols.description: Private exception class defined inside run() solely for the embedder-down degradation test path.

symbols.does: Always raises to simulate embedder failure.
symbols.description: Fake embedder function used to verify that attach_semantic_edges degrades gracefully: it emits a warning, leaves the semantic field absent, and keeps the structural index intact.
